channel.title: Concurrency on mo11om
channel.description: Recent content in Concurrency on mo11om

item.title: IRC Daemon
item.author: user@example.com (Hugo Author)
item.description: The Problem The naive approach to a multi-client server is one thread per connection: accept a client, spawn a thread, read in a loop. This works up to a few hundred clients, then collapses under thread scheduling overhead and per-thread stack allocation. The alternative is I/O multiplexing: one thread, one poll() call, many file descriptors. The operating system tells you which sockets are ready to read. You service them in sequence.
